creator: khera@kcilink.com
text:
>>>>> "MSFF" == Mario Sergio Fujikawa Ferreira <lioux@uol.com.br> writes:

MSFF> 	This is a known artifact and should be documented somewhere on
MSFF> the the clockspeed site.
MSFF> 	A fix is as follows:

MSFF> 	- I'll update the port but you already have the leapsecs.dat
MSFF> installed. Install leapsecs.dat under /usr/local/etc/clockspeed

You need my patch to make the leapseconds file found there.
currently it is looking in /etc/leapsecs.dat.

MSFF> 	- killall clockspeed (you can't have it running)
MSFF> 	- sntpclock IP.OF.NTP.SERVER | clockadd
MSFF> 	- start clockspeed
MSFF> 	- sntpclock IP.OF.NTP.SERVER >
MSFF> /usr/local/etc/clockspeed/adjust
MSFF> 	- cd /usr/src/share/zoneinfo
MSFF> 	- make -DLEAPSECONDS all install

Ahh.  Curiously, if I use taiclockd on a good timekeeper then I can
set the clock properly without the leapseconds breakage.

MSFF> 	Try this and let me know how your system reacts.
MSFF> And... thanks for reminding me of it. :) I'll try to fix this for good
MSFF> this time.

Thanks.  I'll test it out.  I'm assuming that the LEAPSECONDS support
in zoneinfo won't break other things, will it?

It doesn't seem to fix it for me.  It is still trying to set the clock
22 seconds fast.

I'll just use the taiclockd to set the time.
